description_long: This is a highly ornate, antique-style ceramic vase with a bulbous lower body and a narrower cylindrical neck, topped by a decorative lid. The surface is covered in a mottled brown and amber glaze, giving it a speckled, almost animal-print appearance. A prominent, sculpted handle attaches from the upper neck to the shoulder, featuring intricate relief work and a metallic-looking hinge or clasp near the lid. The lid itself is domed and adorned with raised floral or shell-like motifs, suggesting a blend of functional and decorative design. The base is slightly flared, providing stability, and the overall craftsmanship hints at a historical or artisanal origin. This vase would likely serve as both a functional vessel and a decorative centerpiece in an urban or historical setting.
description: A richly glazed, antique-style ceramic vase with a bulbous body, ornate handle, and decorative domed lid, featuring intricate relief work and a mottled brown finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the ornate handle, domed lid with relief motifs, and the mottled brown glaze on the bulbous body.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full curvature of the handle and the hinge detail near the lid, with the body’s rounded form prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the domed lid and the smooth, uninterrupted glazed surface of the vase’s body.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizes the vase’s rounded back and the subtle transition from the neck to the body, with less detail on the handle.
